prevalence.source: Ministério da Saúde / SBI + epidemiological syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; antimicrobial resistance increasing in some regions

incidence.value: 50-100 per 1,000 women/year (any UTI episode; varies by age)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms (dysuria, frequency) plus urinalysis; culture in complicated/recurrent cases; management aligned with IDSA-adapted Brazilian guidance

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening (except bacteriuria screening in pregnancy in many prenatal programs)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High cure rates for uncomplicated cystitis; resistance complicates empiric therapy in some areas

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS diagnosis with urinalysis; culture access variable; prenatal screening for bacteriuria varies
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Nitrofurantoin/fosfomycin or TMP-SMX guided by resistance; avoid fluoroquinolones for uncomplicated cases when possible; pyelonephritis requires broader therapy and follow-up
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Rising E. coli resistance, variable culture access, and high recurrence in some populations
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Wide APS coverage in SUS and availability of core antibiotics
